ddls:
CREATE TABLE "weather_data" (
  "weather_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each weather data entry */,
  "location_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the location where the weather data was collected */,
  "station_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the weather station that collected the data */,
  "forecast_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the forecast associated with the data */,
  "last_update" TEXT /* Date and time of the last update to the weather data */,
  "temperature" REAL /* Current temperature in Celsius */,
  "weather_text" TEXT /* Text description of the current weather conditions */,
  "wind_speed" REAL /* Current wind speed in km/h */,
  "wind_direction" TEXT /* Current wind direction */,
  "precipitation" REAL /* Current precipitation in mm */,
  "humidity" REAL /* Current humidity as a percentage */,
  "cloud_cover" REAL /* Current cloud cover as a percentage */,
  "feel" REAL /* Current feel temperature in Celsius */,
  "visibility" REAL /* Current visibility in km */,
  "uv_index" REAL /* Current UV index */,
  "wind_gust" REAL /* Current wind gust in km/h */,
  "data_source" TEXT /* Source of the weather data */,
  "data_quality" TEXT /* Quality of the weather data */,
  "is_archived" INTEGER /* Boolean to indicate if the data is archived */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("weather_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_weather_data_forecast_id FOREIGN KEY ("forecast_id") REFERENCES forecasts ("forecast_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_weather_data_location_id FOREIGN KEY ("location_id") REFERENCES locations ("location_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_weather_data_station_id FOREIGN KEY ("station_id") REFERENCES weather_stations ("station_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "locations" (
  "location_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each location */,
  "location_name" TEXT /* Name of the location */,
  "latitude" REAL /* Latitude of the location */,
  "longitude" REAL /* Longitude of the location */,
  "country" TEXT /* Country where the location is situated */,
  "region" TEXT /* Region or state where the location is situated */,
  "elevation" REAL /* Elevation of the location in meters */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("location_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "weather_stations" (
  "station_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each weather station */,
  "location_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the location where the station is located */,
  "station_name" TEXT /* Name of the weather station */,
  "station_type" TEXT /* Type of the weather station (e.g., airport, city) */,
  "installation_date" TEXT /* Date when the station was installed */,
  "last_maintenance_date" TEXT /* Date of the last maintenance */,
  "status" TEXT /* Current status of the station (e.g., operational, maintenance) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("station_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_weather_stations_location_id FOREIGN KEY ("location_id") REFERENCES locations ("location_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "weather_types" (
  "weather_type_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each weather type */,
  "weather_type_name" TEXT /* Name of the weather type (e.g., sunny, cloudy, rainy) */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the weather type */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("weather_type_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "wind_directions" (
  "wind_direction_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each wind direction */,
  "wind_direction_name" TEXT /* Name of the wind direction (e.g., north, south, east, west) */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the wind direction */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("wind_direction_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "precipitation_types" (
  "precipitation_type_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each precipitation type */,
  "precipitation_type_name" TEXT /* Name of the precipitation type (e.g., rain, snow, hail) */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the precipitation type */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("precipitation_type_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "user_name" TEXT /* Full name of the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "phone_number" TEXT /* Contact number of the user */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., admin, forecaster, researcher) */,
  "last_login" TEXT /* Date and time of the last login */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Date and time when the user account was created */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Date and time when the user account was last updated */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "alerts" (
  "alert_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each alert */,
  "location_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the location where the alert is issued */,
  "alert_type" TEXT /* Type of the alert (e.g., severe weather, flood warning) */,
  "alert_message" TEXT /* Message of the alert */,
  "start_time" TEXT /* Start time of the alert */,
  "end_time" TEXT /* End time of the alert */,
  "severity_level" TEXT /* Severity level of the alert (e.g., low, medium, high) */,
  "issued_by" INTEGER /* User who issued the alert */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("alert_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_alerts_location_id FOREIGN KEY ("location_id") REFERENCES locations ("location_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_alerts_issued_by FOREIGN KEY ("issued_by") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "alert_log" (
  "log_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each log entry */,
  "alert_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the alert being logged */,
  "location_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the location where the alert was issued */,
  "alert_type" TEXT /* Type of the alert */,
  "alert_message" TEXT /* Message of the alert */,
  "issue_time" TEXT /* Time when the alert was issued */,
  "resolved_by" INTEGER /* User who resolved the alert */,
  "resolution_time" TEXT /* Time when the alert was resolved */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("log_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_alert_log_alert_id FOREIGN KEY ("alert_id") REFERENCES alerts ("alert_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_alert_log_resolved_by FOREIGN KEY ("resolved_by") REFERENCES users ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_alert_log_location_id FOREIGN KEY ("location_id") REFERENCES locations ("location_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "forecasts" (
  "forecast_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each forecast */,
  "location_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the location where the forecast is being made */,
  "forecast_date" TEXT /* Date of the forecast */,
  "forecast_text" TEXT /* Text of the forecast */,
  "forecast_accuracy" TEXT /* Accuracy of the forecast (e.g., high, medium, low) */,
  "issued_by" INTEGER /* User who issued the forecast */,
  "issued_time" TEXT /* Time when the forecast was issued */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("forecast_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_forecasts_issued_by FOREIGN KEY ("issued_by") REFERENCES users ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_forecasts_location_id FOREIGN KEY ("location_id") REFERENCES locations ("location_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users_locations" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the user */,
  "location_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the location authorized for the user */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id", "location_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "forecast_log" (
  "log_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each log entry */,
  "forecast_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the forecast being logged */,
  "location_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the location where the forecast was made */,
  "forecast_date" TEXT /* Date of the forecast */,
  "forecast_text" TEXT /* Text of the forecast */,
  "forecast_accuracy" TEXT /* Accuracy of the forecast (e.g., high, medium, low) */,
  "resolved_by" INTEGER /* User who resolved the forecast */,
  "resolution_time" TEXT /* Time when the forecast was resolved */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("log_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_forecast_log_forecast_id FOREIGN KEY ("forecast_id") REFERENCES forecasts ("forecast_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_forecast_log_resolved_by FOREIGN KEY ("resolved_by") REFERENCES users ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_forecast_log_location_id FOREIGN KEY ("location_id") REFERENCES locations ("location_id")
)
